title: LICHEN_MIN_FRAME_LEN comment could be clearer about what it protects
description:
lichen_l2.c:81-92 documents the minimum frame size but doesn't explain what goes wrong if a shorter frame is processed. The comment says 'matches lichen_frame_parse() in frame.c which rejects frames < 9 bytes' but doesn't say why that matters here.

What goes wrong: A reader might think this is redundant validation and remove it, not realizing it prevents out-of-bounds reads during frame parsing before the MIC check.

Suggested addition:
```c
/*
 * SECURITY: Early rejection of runt frames prevents out-of-bounds reads
 * in lichen_frame_parse() before MIC validation can occur.
 */
```
